_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.**

- **Exact title**: Identifying and Mitigating the Influence of the Prior Distribution in Large Language Models[1][2][3]
- **Authors**: Liyi Zhang, Veniamin Veselovsky, R. Thomas McCoy, Thomas L. Griffiths[2][3]
- **Venue**: arXiv preprint (cs.CL category); listed in Google Scholar under Proceedings of the Annual... (possibly forthcoming conference proceedings)[1][2]
- **Year**: 2025[1][2]
- **arXiv ID**: 2504.12585 (v1 submitted on 17 Apr 2025)[2]
- **DOI/URL**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.12585[2]

The paper investigates how implicit priors in LLMs cause errors on deterministic tasks (e.g., counting), proposing prompting and mechanistic interpretability interventions like layer-specific finetuning to mitigate prior influence[2][3]. No conference acceptance (e.g., NAACL 2025, EMNLP 2025) is confirmed in results[4][5].
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.

**Yes, the paper exists on arXiv.**

- **Exact title**: Identifying and Mitigating the Influence of the Prior Distribution in Large Language Models[1][2]
- **Authors**: Liyi Zhang, Veniamin Veselovsky, R. Thomas McCoy, Thomas L. Griffiths[2]
- **Venue**: arXiv (cs category)[2]
- **Year**: 2025 (submitted on 17 Apr 2025)[2]
- **arXiv ID**: 2504.12585v1[1][2]

The abstract discusses how LLMs fail on deterministic tasks due to token prior distributions, shows prompting and mechanistic interpretability (e.g., logit lens, lightweight finetuning) improve performance on prior-dominated tasks like counting or acronyms, and suggests methods to reduce prior reliance.[1][2][3]
